definition: Combining multiple data points into a single summary value such as a sum, mean, or count.
exampleSentence: The aggregation step groups sales by month and sums the revenue.

definition: Identifying data points that deviate significantly from expected patterns or distributions.
exampleSentence: Anomaly detection caught a server error before the on-call team did.

definition: An ordered collection of elements of the same type, foundational to numerical computing.
exampleSentence: NumPy uses an array structure to do math on millions of numbers fast.

definition: The sum of all values in a dataset divided by the count of those values.
exampleSentence: The arithmetic mean is sensitive to extreme values in the dataset.

definition: An algorithm computing gradients through a neural network to update weights during training.
exampleSentence: Without backpropagation, deep networks could not learn from their mistakes.

definition: A simple reference model used to measure whether a more complex model adds real value.
exampleSentence: The baseline was just predicting the most common class every time.

definition: A subset of training data processed together in one forward and backward pass of a model.
exampleSentence: Processing data in small batches reduces memory pressure during training.

definition: Updating the probability of a hypothesis as new evidence is incorporated using Bayes' theorem.
exampleSentence: Bayesian inference updates our belief about the bug rate with each test result.

definition: Systematic error in a model that causes it to consistently miss the true value in one direction.
exampleSentence: Underfitting models often suffer from high bias toward simple patterns.

definition: A prediction task with exactly two possible output categories, such as spam or not spam.
exampleSentence: Is this transaction fraudulent? That's a binary classification task.

definition: A variable representing discrete groups or labels rather than continuous numeric values.
exampleSentence: Product category is a categorical variable we need to encode before training.

definition: The geometric center of a cluster, used as a reference point in algorithms like k-means.
exampleSentence: K-means moves each centroid until the cluster assignments stop changing.

definition: A dataset condition where one category has significantly more samples than others.
exampleSentence: We oversampled the minority class to address class imbalance in the data.

definition: A supervised learning task that predicts which category a new observation belongs to.
exampleSentence: Sentiment analysis is a classification problem with three possible outputs.

definition: An unsupervised method grouping similar data points together without predefined labels.
exampleSentence: Clustering revealed four distinct usage patterns among our users.

definition: A table comparing predicted vs actual class labels to evaluate a classification model.
exampleSentence: The confusion matrix showed the model confused cats and dogs most often.

definition: A numeric variable that can take any value within a range, such as height or temperature.
exampleSentence: Price is a continuous variable, so we model it with regression, not classification.

definition: A statistical measure of how strongly two variables move together, ranging from -1 to 1.
exampleSentence: A correlation close to zero means the two variables move independently.

definition: A technique splitting data into multiple train-test folds to produce a more reliable evaluation.
exampleSentence: Five-fold cross-validation gave us a more reliable estimate of performance.

definition: Comma-Separated Values — a plain text format for storing tabular data, one row per line.
exampleSentence: The data team dropped a fresh CSV on the shared drive every morning.

definition: The process of detecting and correcting errors, inconsistencies, and missing values in a dataset.
exampleSentence: Data cleaning revealed that ten percent of the rows had duplicate entries.

definition: The gradual change in input data distribution over time that degrades model performance.
exampleSentence: We monitor for data drift so we know when to retrain the model.

definition: A centralized repository storing raw, unstructured data at scale for future processing.
exampleSentence: The data lake stores everything raw so analysts can query it later.

definition: An automated sequence of steps that ingests, transforms, and loads data from source to destination.
exampleSentence: The data pipeline failed because one source changed its column names.

definition: A structured storage system optimized for querying and analyzing large volumes of historical data.
exampleSentence: Analysts run their reports directly against the data warehouse.

definition: A two-dimensional, labeled data structure with columns of potentially different types, as in pandas.
exampleSentence: Filter the DataFrame to keep only rows where the age column is over eighteen.

definition: A flowchart-like model that splits data based on feature conditions to make predictions.
exampleSentence: We used a decision tree because the client wanted to understand each decision.

definition: Reducing the number of features in a dataset while preserving as much information as possible.
exampleSentence: PCA is a common dimensionality reduction technique for tabular data.

definition: A function describing the frequency of values across all possible outcomes in a dataset.
exampleSentence: Check the distribution of each feature before training your model.

definition: Exploratory Data Analysis — the initial investigation of data using statistics and visualizations.
exampleSentence: During EDA we found three columns that were almost entirely null.

definition: One complete pass of the entire training dataset through a machine learning model.
exampleSentence: Stop training after thirty epochs if the validation loss hasn't improved.

definition: Extract, Transform, Load — the process of moving and reshaping data between systems.
exampleSentence: The ETL process runs every hour and feeds fresh data to the dashboard.

definition: A quantitative measure used to assess how well a model performs on a given task.
exampleSentence: Which evaluation metric matters here — accuracy, recall, or AUC?

definition: The harmonic mean of precision and recall, balancing both in a single performance metric.
exampleSentence: We optimize for F1 score because both false positives and negatives are costly.

definition: An individual measurable variable used as input to a machine learning model.
exampleSentence: The most predictive feature turned out to be the time of day.

definition: Creating or transforming input variables to improve a model's predictive power.
exampleSentence: Feature engineering transformed raw timestamps into day-of-week signals.

definition: A score indicating how much each input variable contributed to a model's predictions.
exampleSentence: Feature importance showed that price had twice the influence of color.

definition: An optimization algorithm iteratively adjusting model parameters to minimize a loss function.
exampleSentence: Stochastic gradient descent uses one sample per step instead of the full batch.

definition: The verified correct labels or values used to train and evaluate a machine learning model.
exampleSentence: We collected ground truth by asking human annotators to label the images.

definition: A portion of data withheld from training and used only for final model evaluation.
exampleSentence: The holdout set stays locked until the very final model evaluation.

definition: A configuration value set before training that controls how a model learns, not what it learns.
exampleSentence: We ran a grid search over the key hyperparameters to find the best combination.

definition: A statistical method determining whether observed data provides evidence against a null assumption.
exampleSentence: Hypothesis testing told us the difference in conversion rate was not significant.

definition: Filling in missing values in a dataset using statistical estimates like the mean or median.
exampleSentence: KNN imputation fills in missing values based on similar rows in the dataset.

definition: The phase where a trained model generates predictions on new, unseen input data.
exampleSentence: Inference is fast because we cached the embeddings ahead of time.

definition: The difference between the 75th and 25th percentile, measuring statistical spread robustly.
exampleSentence: We removed points more than 1.5 times the interquartile range from the median.

definition: The target output value a supervised learning model is trained to predict.
exampleSentence: Each label was verified by two independent annotators before use.

definition: A hyperparameter controlling how large each step is during gradient descent optimization.
exampleSentence: Try a lower learning rate if the training loss is bouncing around.

definition: A function measuring the difference between predicted and actual values during model training.
exampleSentence: Choose the right loss function before you write a single line of model code.

definition: The middle value in an ordered dataset, robust to outliers unlike the arithmetic mean.
exampleSentence: The median salary tells you more than the average in a skewed market.

definition: An absent data point in a dataset, represented as null or NaN, requiring handling before modeling.
exampleSentence: A column with thirty percent missing values probably needs a different strategy.

definition: A mathematical function learned from data that maps inputs to predicted outputs.
exampleSentence: We tried five different models and the gradient boosted one won.

definition: A condition where two or more input features are highly correlated, distorting model coefficients.
exampleSentence: Drop one of the correlated features to remove multicollinearity from the model.

definition: A symmetric, bell-shaped probability distribution where most values cluster around the mean.
exampleSentence: Many statistical tests assume the data follows a normal distribution.

definition: Rescaling feature values to a standard range — typically 0 to 1 — before model training.
exampleSentence: Apply normalization so features with large ranges don't dominate the model.

definition: The default assumption of no effect or no difference that a statistical test attempts to reject.
exampleSentence: Our null hypothesis was that the two groups had identical click rates.

definition: A data point that differs markedly from other observations and may indicate noise or a real anomaly.
exampleSentence: Remove each outlier only if you have a good reason, not just because it's extreme.

definition: When a model learns the training data too precisely, performing poorly on new unseen data.
exampleSentence: Overfitting is the single most common mistake in beginner ML projects.

definition: The probability of observing results at least as extreme as those seen, assuming the null hypothesis is true.
exampleSentence: A low p-value doesn't always mean the effect is practically important.

definition: A Python library providing DataFrame structures for data manipulation and analysis.
exampleSentence: Pandas is the first library most data scientists learn after NumPy.

definition: The value below which a given percentage of observations in a dataset fall.
exampleSentence: Response time at the 99th percentile is what affects the worst user experience.

definition: A chained sequence of preprocessing and modeling steps that can be fit and applied as a unit.
exampleSentence: Wrapping everything in an ML pipeline prevents data leakage between steps.

definition: The proportion of positive predictions that were actually correct out of all positive predictions made.
exampleSentence: High precision means you're not wasting time chasing false positives.

definition: The output a trained model generates when given a new input it has not seen before.
exampleSentence: The model's prediction matched the actual outcome in eighty percent of cases.

definition: All data transformation steps applied before feeding data into a machine learning model.
exampleSentence: Preprocessing accounts for about half the time in a real data science project.

definition: An ensemble model combining many decision trees to improve accuracy and reduce overfitting.
exampleSentence: A random forest is usually the best first model to try on tabular data.

definition: The proportion of actual positives that a model correctly identified out of all real positives.
exampleSentence: Improving recall meant the model caught more actual fraud cases.

definition: A supervised learning task predicting a continuous numeric output from input features.
exampleSentence: We used linear regression to estimate next quarter's revenue from past trends.

definition: A technique penalizing model complexity during training to reduce overfitting.
exampleSentence: L2 regularization penalizes large weights and helps prevent overfitting.

definition: A subset of observations drawn from a larger population for analysis or model training.
exampleSentence: A random sample of five percent was large enough to validate the approach.

definition: A systematic error occurring when the collected data does not represent the full population.
exampleSentence: Sampling bias made the model fail on customers from rural areas.

definition: A measure of the asymmetry of a distribution — positive skew has a longer right tail.
exampleSentence: Log-transforming the target reduced skewness and improved model performance.

definition: Dividing a dataset into separate portions for training a model and evaluating its performance.
exampleSentence: Always do your train-test split before any preprocessing to avoid leakage.

definition: A measure of how spread out values are relative to the mean in a dataset.
exampleSentence: Feature values more than three standard deviations out were treated as outliers.

definition: Training a model on labeled data where the correct output is known for each input.
exampleSentence: Most business prediction problems are solved with supervised learning.

definition: The output column a model is trained to predict, also called the dependent variable.
exampleSentence: The target variable is the churn flag we created from the subscription data.

definition: The labeled dataset used to fit a machine learning model's parameters.
exampleSentence: Without enough training data, even the best model will struggle.

definition: When a model is too simple to capture patterns in the data, performing poorly on both train and test sets.
exampleSentence: If train and test accuracy are both low, the model is probably underfitting.

definition: Training a model on unlabeled data to discover hidden structure or patterns without guidance.
exampleSentence: Unsupervised learning is useful when you don't know what you're looking for yet.

definition: A model's sensitivity to fluctuations in training data — high variance models tend to overfit.
exampleSentence: A model with low bias and high variance is memorizing the training set.

definition: A one-dimensional array of numbers representing a data point or feature set in a model.
exampleSentence: The embedding model turns each sentence into a dense vector of numbers.

definition: Representing data graphically to reveal patterns, trends, and outliers at a glance.
exampleSentence: Data visualization showed the seasonal pattern that the model had missed.

definition: A learned parameter in a model that scales the importance of an input feature.
exampleSentence: After training, the model assigns a high weight to the most useful features.
